question_text: Why does pressure in a liquid increase as you go deeper underwater?

options.label: A
options.text: The deeper you go, the colder the water becomes, which increases pressure.

options.label: B
options.text: There is more water above you pushing down, so the weight of the water exerts greater pressure.

options.label: C
options.text: The water molecules move faster at greater depths, creating more pressure.

options.label: D
options.text: The pressure increases because the area of the ocean floor gets smaller at depth.

explanation: Liquid pressure increases with depth because the weight of all the water above pushes down on the water below. This is captured in the formula P = ρgh, where the pressure depends on the depth (h). Temperature and molecular speed do not cause the increase; it is the weight of the water column above that matters.
